summary: Reigning world champions and the model's clear favourite (champ 24.9% vs a 9% market). A settled, tournament-hardened core under Lionel Scaloni built around the best player of his generation.
style: Flexible 4-3-3 / 4-4-2, comfortable controlling games through midfield but lethal in transition; compact mid-block out of possession.

counterReliance.note: Mid-block into counter — finds Messi & Álvarez vertically and fast

strengths: Elite tournament experience and a winning spine that has been together since 2021
Midfield control with De Paul / Mac Allister / Fernández dictating tempo
Match-winners in the final third who decide tight knockout games
weaknesses: Ageing in key areas — fitness over a seven-game run is a question
Full-back depth thinner than the rest of the squad

keyPlayers.name: Lionel Messi
keyPlayers.note: The model's single biggest swing factor — creation and finishing in one player

keyPlayers.name: Alexis Mac Allister
keyPlayers.note: Two-way engine linking defence to attack

tactics: Happy to let lesser sides have the ball and strike on the counter, but can also patiently break down a low block through Messi drifting into pockets. Set-piece threat from a tall back line. The plan rarely changes; the personnel adapt to the opponent.
mismatch: Vulnerable to high-energy pressing sides that deny the midfield time; dominant against teams that try to play open, end-to-end football.
xFactor: Messi's fitness and minutes management across a 39-day tournament.
notes: Edit or delete this file freely — it is here as a worked example of the knowledge-base schema (see _template.json).
